title: Shapes of Stories
author: Kurt Vonnegut
source: From Vonnegut's rejected University of Chicago anthropology master's thesis (1947); popularized in lectures.

lineage.note: Plots graphed as lines on a good-fortune / ill-fortune axis. Philosophy: emotional TRAJECTORY matters more than event taxonomy. The most useful lens for documentary because it's about felt shape, not plot mechanics.

stages.name: Man in Hole
stages.function: Protagonist starts at neutral, falls into trouble, climbs out, ends higher than they started.

stages.name: Boy Meets Girl
stages.function: Finds something wonderful, loses it, regains it.

stages.name: Cinderella
stages.function: Step-rise (incremental gains), sudden collapse, then off-the-chart happiness.

stages.name: From Bad to Worse
stages.function: Starts low and gets worse — Kafka's territory.

stages.name: Creation Story
stages.function: Starts in chaos / void and ends in stable order.

stages.name: Old Testament
stages.function: Series of falls and partial recoveries; non-monotonic.

stages.name: New Testament
stages.function: Creation Story plus a Cinderella spike at the end — off-the-chart happiness after order is established.

cross_framework_equivalence._note: Vonnegut shapes are orthogonal to beat-ladder frameworks; they describe emotional trajectory and can be overlaid on any of them.

applicability.limitations: Doesn't tell you WHAT scenes to use — only whether the cumulative emotional shape is what you wanted. A diagnostic, not a template.
